records.quote: Electronically signed: Tunde Okafor, OD — 11/21/2022

Medication history is notable for medroxyprogesterone acetate (Depo-Provera) 150 mg IM every 12–13 weeks from 04/13/2021 through 07/19/2022 (six documented injections, per patient and available records).

Imaging: MRI brain with and without contrast and MRV (12/02/2022, Springfield Imaging Center): no mass, no hydrocephalus, no venous sinus thrombosis. Partially empty sella and posterior globe flattening noted — findings compatible with elevated intracranial pressure.

data.intervention: MRI brain with and without contrast + MRV
data.result: No mass, no hydrocephalus, no venous sinus thrombosis; partially empty sella and posterior globe flattening — compatible with elevated intracranial pressure

records.quote: Literature reports an association between progestin-only injectable contraception and intracranial hypertension in some patients; given the temporal relationship here, the injectable contraceptive is a potentially contributing factor and should be discontinued in coordination with her gynecologist.

data.statement: Literature reports an association between progestin-only injectable contraception and intracranial hypertension in some patients; given the temporal relationship here, the injectable contraceptive is a potentially contributing factor and should be discontinued in coordination with her gynecologist.
data.author: Priya Natarajan, MD

records.quote: Impression: Clinical picture highly suggestive of idiopathic intracranial hypertension (pseudotumor cerebri, G93.2).
